name: Doublecrust Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: Sour Cream Dough for a 2-crust pie -- above
3 pounds firm, tart apples (such as Granny Smith, Pippin, or Golden Delicious)
Egg wash: 1 egg well beaten with a pinch of salt
1 teaspoon sugar for finishing the top of the pie, optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel, halve, and core the apples. Cut each half into 5 or 6 wedges.
2. Melt the butter in a wide, shallow, slopingsided pan over medium heat. When the butter begins to sizzle, add the sliced apples and cook, tossing gently until they begin to sizzle about 1 minute. Add the lemon juice and sugar and continue to toss gently until the apples are tender but still fairly firm for about 5 minutes longer.
3. Pour the filling into a nonreactive pan or a pan lined with plastic wrap so the apple slices fit in a single layer. Sprinkle with the cinnamon and let cool.
4. Set a rack at the upper third and lower third of the oven and preheat to 400°F.
5. Roll out the bottom crust and arrange in the pan. Pour the cooled filling into the bottom crust. Roll out the top crust for double-crust pie.
6. Cut several slashes in the top crust and carefully brush with egg wash. Be careful not to leave puddles of liquid on the crust.
7. If desired, sprinkle the top crust with sugar.
8. Place the pie in the oven on the lower rack and lower the temperature to 375°F. Bake the pie for about 40 minutes until the crust is baked through and a deep golden brown and the filling is gently bubbling. If the top crust has not colored sufficiently after 30 minutes, move the pie to the upper rack of the oven for the last 10 minutes.
9. Cool the pie on a rack.
Yield: One 9-inch pie
